title: Pleopeltis ovata
taxonomy: Pleopeltis ovata is a species of epiphytic or terrestrial fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Mexico and Central America. The classification of Pleopeltis ovata includes the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Polypodiaceae, genus Pleopeltis, and species P. ovata. The genus Pleopeltis is a group of ferns commonly known as resurrection ferns due to their ability to revive after drying out. This unique trait is a result of the fern's adaptation to survive in areas with limited water availability.
uses: Pleopeltis ovata has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Central and South America. Its leaves are often applied topically to treat various skin conditions, including wounds and eczema. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Traditionally, it has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Pleopeltis ovata.

intro: Pleopeltis ovata, also known as the ovate polypody fern, is a species of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage.
